current_beat: Mateo auditing the control code. Yuki-Mateo first meeting at a technical review.

relationships.yuki-rodrigo: Trusted colleague; first person she showed the data to
relationships.mateo-yuki: First meeting at technical review. Mutual recognition — two plumbers who read the same pipes.
relationships.renata-yuki: Professional contact; Renata called Yuki about residuals
relationships.renata-ephrem: First meeting in Geneva. Mutual recognition of rigor. She showed the data; he named the shape.
relationships.ephrem-yara: Three-hour dispute at Atacama. Mutual respect intact. The possibility of sharing a frame of reference — broken.

events: T1: Yuki notices anomalous residuals in the Rubin pipeline. Logs them, keeps working.
T2: Yuki shows residuals to Rodrigo Vásquez. Fourth night confirms.
T3: Mateo at Paranal — ordinary day. Seo-yun in passing. Rubin rumor heard, forgotten.
T4: Renata models object (density 5.5 = Earth). Calls Yuki. Ephrem identifies constrained topology.

T5: Information leaks. Press calls it a 'giant asteroid'. Yuki interviewed, described as 'scientist who discovered the object'. She hates it. Goes home, passes the spare bedroom, doesn't open the box. Makes coffee.

T6: Official confirmation — multiple observatories, UN briefed, 30-year timeline. Renata remodels interior for the 5th time (Earth signature converges within 2σ). She tells no one. Ephrem invited to international working group as principal mathematical consultant; writes 'Répondre demain' on the letter (meaning yes). Yuki attends first committee meeting as 'technical consultant'; speaks once, factually; turns her badge face-down on the counter.

T7: Geneva, working group plenary. Renata presents Earth-signature data to core team in closed session. Twelve slides. Ephrem connects his compact manifold topology to Renata's data: 'un objet qui revient' — the closed loop, the paradox. The word is spoken. The sheet with 5.43-5.57 g/cm³ stays on the table between them.

T8: Manchester, Schuster Building lab. Kwame called to rule out RF interference on a teleportation experiment. Checks signal chain, runs own RFI sweep. Signal arrives 0.3ms before transmission — reproducible. He confirms: 'Votre signal est propre. Ce n'est pas de l'interférence.' Drives home to Goostrey. Jollof rice. Prays in Twi. Sits alone at the table.

T9: Atacama lab forming. Yara arrives — walks the prototype testing area, observes cables and systems, asks her first diagnostic question. Nadia in Vienna finishes her 6-page energy assessment (412 reactors, global nuclear fleet + additional capacity). Sends it. Calls Lesia Sunday — talks about rain, Pavlo's maquette, nothing about the list. THE 0.9743 IS PLANTED: unnamed calibration engineer writes correction factor into config file for prototype geometry (43° arc). Test passes. Number undocumented. Engineer moves on.

T10: Paradox goes public. Ephrem in Addis Ababa draws the closed surface on his whiteboard — compact manifold, no boundary, no origin. The world debates; both sides cite his topology. He knows it proves neither — it proves the distinction itself may not exist. Yuki in La Serena runs the coast road, doesn't make forty minutes. Her mother asked about the box. She said 'bientôt.' Three years.

T11: Atacama complex, safety architecture design review. Ephrem stays after the room empties. He shows Yara that her safety architecture assumes a boundary between 'not activated' and 'activated successfully' that his topology says may not exist — topologically indistinguishable, the same point on the manifold. Yara understands immediately: her architecture is not wrong, it is 'aveugle à quelque chose de fondamental dans la structure du problème.' Ephrem deepens: the indecidability is structural, not epistemic. Two diagrams on the whiteboard — Yara's architecture and Ephrem's surface. Nobody erases them.

T12: Consortium plenary. The fracture widens beyond the team. Jeanne Moreau-Blanc (former fluid mechanics engineer) speaks against — precise technical objection about tolerance margins that becomes something larger. Père Gabriel Okonkwo (theologian, Gregorian Rome) asks: 'Qui a décidé que cette décision nous appartenait?' Amir Darwish (political coordinator) ratifies construction — understands political cost of inaction. Yuki watches from the back row, badge face-down. Ephrem in Addis reads the transcript, recognizes Okonkwo's question as the theological version of his topological result. The vote passes. The project continues.

T13: Life continues. Renata swims at Piscine Joséphine Baker — counts laps, the density number follows her into the water (5.51, five iterations). Ephrem waters his lemon tree in Addis — small sour triumphant fruits, the mathematics don't vote. Kwame in Goostrey composes unsent letters to Gifty in his head — 'the effect can precede the cause, I would have written sooner if I had known how' — the letter dissolves. Yuki runs the coast road (44:12, still can't break 40), calls her mother. Her mother asks about the box. 'Bientôt.' The word holds for one more week.

T14: Year 6. Mateo called to the Atacama complex — his VLT control software has been adapted, rewritten, made unrecognizable for the planetary teleportation system. Calls his mother (silence), his father takes the phone ('c'est un travail important'). Packs. The bracelet stays on. He drives south in the morning. The code is somewhere ahead, in a form no one has shown him yet.

T15: Year 7. Technical review at the complex. Mateo asks about an undocumented correction factor (1.00417) in the calibration code — Delgado's Thursday hotfix for thermal drift. Yuki, there as data pipeline consultant, recognizes the question instantly — she'd seen the same thing the night before. They exchange commit hashes and dates. The room watches two plumbers speak a language it doesn't speak. Mateo's workaround file: 87 entries. The 0.9743 is deeper in the code. He hasn't found it yet.
